file.content:
-- $Id: testes/vararg.lua $
-- See Copyright Notice in file all.lua

print('testing vararg')

local function f (a, ...)
  local x = {n = select('#', ...), ...}
  for i = 1, x.n do assert(a[i] == x[i]) end
  return x.n
end

local function c12 (...)
  assert(arg == _G.arg)    -- no local 'arg'
  local x = {...}; x.n = #x
  local res = (x.n==2 and x[1] == 1 and x[2] == 2)
  if res then res = 55 end
  return res, 2
end

local function vararg (...) return {n = select('#', ...), ...} end

local call = function (f, args) return f(table.unpack(args, 1, args.n)) end

assert(f() == 0)
assert(f({1,2,3}, 1, 2, 3) == 3)
assert(f({"alo", nil, 45, f, nil}, "alo", nil, 45, f, nil) == 5)

assert(vararg().n == 0)
assert(vararg(nil, nil).n == 2)

assert(c12(1,2)==55)
local a,b = assert(call(c12, {1,2}))
assert(a == 55 and b == 2)
a = call(c12, {1,2;n=2})
assert(a == 55 and b == 2)
a = call(c12, {1,2;n=1})
assert(not a)
assert(c12(1,2,3) == false)
local a = vararg(call(next, {_G,nil;n=2}))
local b,c = next(_G)
assert(a[1] == b and a[2] == c and a.n == 2)
a = vararg(call(call, {c12, {1,2}}))
assert(a.n == 2 and a[1] == 55 and a[2] == 2)
a = call(print, {'+'})
assert(a == nil)

local t = {1, 10}
function t:f (...) local arg = {...}; return self[...]+#arg end
assert(t:f(1,4) == 3 and t:f(2) == 11)
print('+')

print('+')

local lim = 20
local i, a = 1, {}
while i <= lim do a[i] = i+0.3; i=i+1 end

function f(a, b, c, d, ...)
  local more = {...}
  assert(a == 1.3 and more[1] == 5.3 and
         more[lim-4] == lim+0.3 and not more[lim-3])
end

local function g (a,b,c)
  assert(a == 1.3 and b == 2.3 and c == 3.3)
end

call(f, a)
call(g, a)

a = {}
i = 1
while i <= lim do a[i] = i; i=i+1 end
assert(call(math.max, a) == lim)

print("+")


-- new-style varargs

local function oneless (a, ...) return ... end

function f (n, a, ...)
  local b
  assert(arg == _G.arg)   -- no local 'arg'
  if n == 0 then
    local b, c, d = ...
    return a, b, c, d, oneless(oneless(oneless(...)))
  else
    n, b, a = n-1, ..., a
    assert(b == ...)
    return f(n, a, ...)
  end
end

a,b,c,d,e = assert(f(10,5,4,3,2,1))
assert(a==5 and b==4 and c==3 and d==2 and e==1)

a,b,c,d,e = f(4)
assert(a==nil and b==nil and c==nil and d==nil and e==nil)


-- varargs for main chunks
local f = load[[ return {...} ]]
local x = f(2,3)
assert(x[1] == 2 and x[2] == 3 and x[3] == undef)


f = load[[
  local x = {...}
  for i=1,select('#', ...) do assert(x[i] == select(i, ...)) end
  assert(x[select('#', ...)+1] == undef)
  return true
]]

assert(f("a", "b", nil, {}, assert))
assert(f())

a = {select(3, table.unpack{10,20,30,40})}
assert(#a == 2 and a[1] == 30 and a[2] == 40)
a = {select(1)}
assert(next(a) == nil)
a = {select(-1, 3, 5, 7)}
assert(a[1] == 7 and a[2] == undef)
a = {select(-2, 3, 5, 7)}
assert(a[1] == 5 and a[2] == 7 and a[3] == undef)
pcall(select, 10000)
pcall(select, -10000)


-- bug in 5.2.2

function f(p1, p2, p3, p4, p5, p6, p7, p8, p9, p10,
p11, p12, p13, p14, p15, p16, p17, p18, p19, p20,
p21, p22, p23, p24, p25, p26, p27, p28, p29, p30,
p31, p32, p33, p34, p35, p36, p37, p38, p39, p40,
p41, p42, p43, p44, p45, p46, p48, p49, p50, ...)
  local a1,a2,a3,a4,a5,a6,a7
  local a8,a9,a10,a11,a12,a13,a14
end

-- assertion fail here
f()

-- missing arguments in tail call
do
  local function f(a,b,c) return c, b end
  local function g() return f(1,2) end
  local a, b = g()
  assert(a == nil and b == 2)
end

1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
3	#
4	# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
5	# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
6	# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
7	# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
8	# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
9	# context.
10	#
11	# Usage:
12	#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua
13	
14	set -uo pipefail
15	
16	ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
17	cd "$ROOT"
18	
19	TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
20	BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
21	OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
22	mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"
23	
24	if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
25	    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
26	    exit 2
27	fi
28	
29	TEST_FILE="$1"
30	if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
31	    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
32	    exit 2
33	fi
34	
35	if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
36	    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
37	    exit 2
38	fi
39	
40	BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
41	COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
42	OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"
43	
44	PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
45	_soft = true
46	_port = true
47	_nomsg = true
48	_U = false
49	arg = arg or {}
50	_G = _G or _ENV
51	if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
52	'
53	
54	{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"
55	
56	run_with_timeout() {
57	    local src_file="$1"
58	    local out_file="$2"
59	    local src
60	    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
61	    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
62	        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
63	    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
64	        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
65	    else
66	        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
67	        local pid=$!
68	        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &
69	        local watcher=$!
70	        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
71	        local rc=$?
72	        kill "$watcher" 2>/dev/null
73	        return $rc
74	    fi
75	}
76	
77	run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true
78	
79	reached_exec=0
80	grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1
81	
82	if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
83	    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
84	    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
85	    exit 0
86	fi
87	
88	if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
89	    cause="stub"
90	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
91	    cause="parse-panic"
92	elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
93	    cause="codegen-panic"
94	elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
95	    cause="runtime-panic"
96	elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
97	    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
98	        cause="runtime-error"
99	    else
100	        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
101	    fi
102	else
103	    cause="unknown"
104	fi
105	
106	printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
107	echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
108	{
109	    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
110	        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
111	} | head -n 5
112	echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
113	exit 1
114

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# run_official_test.sh <test_file>
#
# Wraps a single Lua 5.4 official test file (reference/lua-c/testes/*.lua)
# with a small preamble that defines the globals those tests assume the
# launcher set up (e.g. _soft, _port, _nomsg, arg) and feeds the whole
# thing into the lua-rs CLI. Captures stdout+stderr, prints a one-line
# PASS/FAIL summary, and (on failure) the first 5 lines of failure
# context.
#
# Usage:
#   ./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/vararg.lua

set -uo pipefail

ROOT="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")/.." && pwd)"
cd "$ROOT"

TEST_TIMEOUT_S=${TEST_TIMEOUT_S:-30}
BIN="$ROOT/target/debug/lua-rs"
OUT_DIR="harness/impl/official"
mkdir -p "$OUT_DIR"

if [ $# -lt 1 ]; then
    echo "usage: $0 <test_file>" >&2
    exit 2
fi

TEST_FILE="$1"
if [ ! -f "$TEST_FILE" ]; then
    echo "[err] test file not found: $TEST_FILE" >&2
    exit 2
fi

if [ ! -x "$BIN" ]; then
    echo "[err] lua-rs binary not built at $BIN" >&2
    exit 2
fi

BASE="$(basename "$TEST_FILE" .lua)"
COMBINED="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.combined.lua"
OUTFILE="$OUT_DIR/$BASE.out"

PREAMBLE='-- harness preamble: emulate the globals lua-c testes/all.lua sets
_soft = true
_port = true
_nomsg = true
_U = false
arg = arg or {}
_G = _G or _ENV
if _VERSION == nil then _VERSION = "Lua 5.4" end
'

{ printf '%s\n' "$PREAMBLE"; cat "$TEST_FILE"; } > "$COMBINED"

run_with_timeout() {
    local src_file="$1"
    local out_file="$2"
    local src
    src="$(cat "$src_file")"
    if command -v gtimeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        gtimeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    elif command -v timeout >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        timeout --signal=KILL "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1
    else
        ( "$BIN" "$src" > "$out_file" 2>&1 ) &
        local pid=$!
        ( sleep "$TEST_TIMEOUT_S" && kill -9 "$pid" 2>/dev/null ) &
        local watcher=$!
        wait "$pid" 2>/dev/null
        local rc=$?
        kill "$watcher" 2>/dev/null
        return $rc
    fi
}

run_with_timeout "$COMBINED" "$OUTFILE" || true

reached_exec=0
grep -qE "^\[4/4\] Executing chunk" "$OUTFILE" && reached_exec=1

if grep -qE "^\[ok\] execution completed" "$OUTFILE" \
    && ! grep -qE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    printf "PASS  %s\n" "$TEST_FILE"
    exit 0
fi

if grep -qE "not yet implemented:" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="stub"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*parser" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="parse-panic"
elif grep -qE "panicked at .*codegen|panicked at .*compil" "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="codegen-panic"
elif grep -qE "^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at " "$OUTFILE"; then
    cause="runtime-panic"
elif grep -qE "^\[err\]" "$OUTFILE"; then
    if [ "$reached_exec" = "1" ]; then
        cause="runtime-error"
    else
        cause="parse-or-compile-error"
    fi
else
    cause="unknown"
fi

printf "FAIL  %s  (cause=%s, reached_exec=%s)\n" "$TEST_FILE" "$cause" "$reached_exec"
echo "--- first 5 lines of failure context ---"
{
    grep -nE "not yet implemented:|^thread '[^']+' .* panicked at |^\[err\]|panicked at " "$OUTFILE" \
        || tail -n 20 "$OUTFILE"
} | head -n 5
echo "--- (full output at $OUTFILE) ---"
exit 1

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/vararg_test1.lua
local function f (a, ...)
  local x = {n = select('#', ...), ...}
  print("n =", x.n, "x[1] =", x[1], "x[2] =", x[2], "x[3] =", x[3])
  for i = 1, x.n do
    print("i =", i, "a[i] =", a[i], "x[i] =", x[i])
    assert(a[i] == x[i])
  end
  return x.n
end

assert(f() == 0)
print("test 1 passed")
assert(f({1,2,3}, 1, 2, 3) == 3)
print("test 2 passed")
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs "$(cat /tmp/vararg_test1.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -30

local function f (a, ...)
  local x = {n = select('#', ...), ...}
  print("n =", x.n, "x[1] =", x[1], "x[2] =", x[2], "x[3] =", x[3])
  for i = 1, x.n do
    print("i =", i, "a[i] =", a[i], "x[i] =", x[i])
    assert(a[i] == x[i])
  end
  return x.n
end

assert(f() == 0)
print("test 1 passed")
assert(f({1,2,3}, 1, 2, 3) == 3)
print("test 2 passed")

  local x = {n = select('#', ...), ...}
  print("n =", x.n, "x[1] =", x[1], "x[2] =", x[2], "x[3] =", x[3])
  for i = 1, x.n do

content.content:
1	//! Lua virtual machine — port of `src/lvm.c` (1899 lines, 32 functions).
2	//!
3	//! This module implements:
4	//! - Number coercion helpers (tonumber_, flttointeger, tointegerns, tointeger)
5	//! - Numeric `for`-loop preparation and stepping (forlimit, forprep, floatforloop)
6	//! - Table get/set with metamethod chaining (finishget, finishset)
7	//! - String comparison respecting embedded NULs (l_strcmp)
8	//! - Relational operators: lessthan, lessequal, equalobj (with metamethods)
9	//! - String concatenation (concat)
10	//! - Object length operator (objlen)
11	//! - Integer arithmetic: idiv, mod, modf, shiftl
12	//! - Closure creation (pushclosure)
13	//! - Yield-resume bridge (finishOp)
14	//! - Main interpreter loop (execute) — the Lua bytecode dispatch engine.
15	//!
16	//! # Control flow note
17	//! The C source uses `goto startfunc` / `goto returning` / `goto ret` across
18	//! labelled points in `luaV_execute`. These are modelled with Rust's labelled
19	//! loops (`'startfunc`, `'returning`, `'dispatch`) and `continue`/`break`
20	//! on those labels.  See inline `PORT NOTE` comments.
21	
22	// C: #include "lprefix.h"
23	// C: #include "lua.h"
24	// C: #include "ldebug.h" "ldo.h" "lfunc.h" "lgc.h" "lobject.h"
25	// C: #include "lopcodes.h" "lstate.h" "lstring.h" "ltable.h" "ltm.h" "lvm.h"
26	
27	#[allow(unused_imports)] use crate::prelude::*;
28	use lua_types::{
29	    CallInfoIdx, GcRef, LuaError, LuaValue, StackIdx,
30	};
31	use lua_types::tagmethod::TagMethod;
32	use lua_types::opcode::Instruction;
33	use crate::state::LuaState;
34	
35	/// TODO(phase-b): lua-types does not yet expose `OpCode`. Stubbed locally with
36	/// all 5.4 opcodes so call sites in vm.rs/debug.rs resolve; the real numeric
37	/// values and per-opcode mode flags live in `lua-types/src/opcode.rs` once
38	/// translated.
39	#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
40	#[allow(non_camel_case_types)]
41	pub enum OpCode {
42	    Move, LoadI, LoadF, LoadK, LoadKX, LoadKx, LoadFalse, LFalseSkip, LoadTrue, LoadNil,
43	    GetUpVal, GetUpval, SetUpVal, GetTabUp, GetTable, GetI, GetField, SetTabUp, SetTable, SetI, SetField,
44	    NewTable, Self_,
45	    AddI, AddK, SubK, MulK, ModK, PowK, DivK, IDivK, BAndK, BOrK, BXOrK,
46	    Add, Sub, Mul, Mod, Pow, Div, IDiv, BAnd, BOr, BXOr, Shl, Shr, ShlI, ShrI,
47	    MmBin, MmBinI, MmBinK,
48	    Unm, BNot, Not, Len, Concat,
49	    Close, Tbc, Jmp,
50	    Eq, Lt, Le, EqK, EqI, LtI, LeI, GtI, GeI, Test, TestSet,

delta.text:
compile errors. Build is green.

**Root cause:** `equal_obj` in `crates/l
LuaClosure` values with `std::ptr::eq(f1 as *const _, f2 as *const _)`. The references `f1` and `f2`
are addresses of the function fields inside two distinct `LuaValue` enum instances (cloned to loc
als before the call), so the pointer comparison always returned false — even when both held the same clos
ure. That made `f == f` evaluate to `false` for any Lua function, breaking the `assert(a[i] == x
[i])` in `vararg.lua` line 8 when `a[i]` and `x[i]` both held the same

function `f`.

**The fix:** In `vm.rs`, properly destructure `LuaClosure` and
compare by `GcRef::ptr_eq` for `Lua`/`C` variants and `==` for the `LightC` registry index.
Mirrored the same logic in `lua-types/src/value.rs` (PartialEq for L

uaValue and `lua_key_eq` for table keys) via a new `closure_eq` helper, so function values used as table keys also work.

**New state:**
I have not re-run the test program per the instructions ("Do NOT run the program at the end"); the
mega-loop will re-test. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green. Ear
lier intermediate runs showed function-identity `==` returning `false` even when both oper
ands printed identical addresses, which the fix corrects.

content.text:
No compile errors. Build is green.

**Root cause:** `equal_obj` in `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:820-824` compared two `LuaClosure` values with `std::ptr::eq(f1 as *const _, f2 as *const _)`. The references `f1` and `f2` are addresses of the function fields inside two distinct `LuaValue` enum instances (cloned to locals before the call), so the pointer comparison always returned false — even when both held the same closure. That made `f == f` evaluate to `false` for any Lua function, breaking the `assert(a[i] == x[i])` in `vararg.lua` line 8 when `a[i]` and `x[i]` both held the same function `f`.

**The fix:** In `vm.rs`, properly destructure `LuaClosure` and compare by `GcRef::ptr_eq` for `Lua`/`C` variants and `==` for the `LightC` registry index. Mirrored the same logic in `lua-types/src/value.rs` (PartialEq for LuaValue and `lua_key_eq` for table keys) via a new `closure_eq` helper, so function values used as table keys also work.

**New state:** I have not re-run the test program per the instructions ("Do NOT run the program at the end"); the mega-loop will re-test. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green. Earlier intermediate runs showed function-identity `==` returning `false` even when both operands printed identical addresses, which the fix corrects.

result:
No compile errors. Build is green.

**Root cause:** `equal_obj` in `crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs:820-824` compared two `LuaClosure` values with `std::ptr::eq(f1 as *const _, f2 as *const _)`. The references `f1` and `f2` are addresses of the function fields inside two distinct `LuaValue` enum instances (cloned to locals before the call), so the pointer comparison always returned false — even when both held the same closure. That made `f == f` evaluate to `false` for any Lua function, breaking the `assert(a[i] == x[i])` in `vararg.lua` line 8 when `a[i]` and `x[i]` both held the same function `f`.

**The fix:** In `vm.rs`, properly destructure `LuaClosure` and compare by `GcRef::ptr_eq` for `Lua`/`C` variants and `==` for the `LightC` registry index. Mirrored the same logic in `lua-types/src/value.rs` (PartialEq for LuaValue and `lua_key_eq` for table keys) via a new `closure_eq` helper, so function values used as table keys also work.

**New state:** I have not re-run the test program per the instructions ("Do NOT run the program at the end"); the mega-loop will re-test. `cargo build -q -p lua-cli` is green. Earlier intermediate runs showed function-identity `==` returning `false` even when both operands printed identical addresses, which the fix corrects.

tool_input.command:
cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/vararg_test1.lua
local function f (a, ...)
  local x = {n = select('#', ...), ...}
  print("n =", x.n, "x[1] =", x[1], "x[2] =", x[2], "x[3] =", x[3])
  for i = 1, x.n do
    print("i =", i, "a[i] =", a[i], "x[i] =", x[i])
    assert(a[i] == x[i])
  end
  return x.n
end

assert(f() == 0)
print("test 1 passed")
assert(f({1,2,3}, 1, 2, 3) == 3)
print("test 2 passed")
EOF
target/debug/lua-rs "$(cat /tmp/vararg_test1.lua)" 2>&1 | tail -30
